title: Unreal Engine 5.7 Scene Restoration
summary: Inside an Unreal Engine 5.7 project with a removed region, restore the missing portion of the scene so it matches a set of reference views — reusing existing project assets without authoring new ones from scratch — and submit a complete Unreal project containing the restored submission map.
taskPrompt:
You are a technical level artist working in Unreal Engine 5.7.

Your task is partial scene restoration, not asset authoring from scratch.

Task:
- Restore the removed scene region using existing Unreal project assets so the scene matches the provided reference views.

Agent-visible files:
- Scene reference images: `input/scene_reference_images`
- Input manifest: `input/manifest.json`
- Input Unreal project: `input/project/SummerRiding2.uproject`

Scene entry:
- Open the scaffold project and load the variant-specific scaffold map.

Requirements:
- Reuse assets that already exist in the visible Unreal project.
- Restore the removed scene region so the scene matches the provided reference views.
- Preserve the visible environment outside the removed region unless a local adjustment is required to integrate the restoration cleanly.
- Keep the fixed task cameras (`TaskCam_*`) available for preview.

Submission contract:
- Submit a complete Unreal project at `output/submission/project/SummerRiding2.uproject`.
- The submitted project must contain the restored map asset at `/Game/Project/Scenes/Submission/final`.
agentMustDo: Open `input/project/SummerRiding2.uproject` in Unreal Engine 5.7 and load the variant's scaffold map.
Reuse existing project assets to restore the removed region; do not author new assets from scratch.
Match the supplied `input/scene_reference_images` views as closely as possible without disturbing the rest of the scene.
Preserve the `TaskCam_*` fixed cameras so the evaluator can render the canonical views.
Submit a complete Unreal project at `output/submission/project/SummerRiding2.uproject` containing the restored map at `/Game/Project/Scenes/Submission/final`.

inputFiles.name: manifest.json
inputFiles.description: Per-variant input manifest describing scaffold map, camera set, and submission map asset path.

inputFiles.name: scene_reference_images/
inputFiles.description: Reference renders of the target scene from the fixed task cameras.

inputFiles.name: project/SummerRiding2.uproject
inputFiles.description: Input Unreal project with the removed region; agent edits a copy in the submission directory.

referenceFiles.name: Submitted Unreal project
referenceFiles.description: Complete Unreal project containing the restored map at `/Game/Project/Scenes/Submission/final`.

outputContracts.name: Submission map asset path
outputContracts.description: Per-variant manifest declares the required submission map asset path (e.g. `/Game/Project/Scenes/Submission/final`).

evaluation: Evaluator enables the required render plugins on the submitted project (MovieRenderPipeline, PythonScriptPlugin, SequencerScripting), launches a headless `UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe` render job that produces a candidate render per `TaskCam_*`, waits for the render report, and downloads the candidate frames. A local soft-eval (`scripts/local_render_eval.run_local_soft_eval`) compares each candidate frame to its reference render and returns a single score in [0,1]. Missing submission, missing submission map asset, render timeouts, or local-eval failures all force 0.0.

classifier.rationale: Unreal Engine scene restoration with rendered camera comparison is 3D / interactive-media authoring.
